top_3_leverage: Delete or replace the base prompt `<soul>` block so it cannot override the already-good authority, repo-safety, verification, and read-only routing contracts.
Add prompt-render regression coverage for conditional variables that depend on tool availability (`search_tool_bm25`, `todo_write`, `git.*`) and fix the render call data or guards accordingly.

Unify subagent/plan-mode output and tool contracts: yield-only subagents should not be told to return plain response text, and prompts should not name shell `tail`/`grep` where `read`/`search` are canonical.
positives: The goal prompts are backed by `GoalRuntime.renderGoalPrompt`, which escapes the user-provided objective before interpolation and preserves usage counters.
RLM research mode's core claims are mostly implementation-backed: a custom `python` tool and `complete_research` tool are registered, and the RLM allowlist includes the tools named by the prompt.
The memory prompts correctly frame local memory as advisory rather than authoritative, and the consolidation path validates parsed JSON before applying artifacts.
The CI-green prompt's preferred `github`/`run_watch` path is backed by a real discoverable `GithubTool` with `op: "run_watch"`.
Most plan-mode active, autoresearch, and memory template variables were verified against their render call data and did not show missing-variable bugs.

findings.title: Remove the conflicting `<soul>` override from the base prompt
findings.body: `system-prompt.md` first establishes scoped routing/repo-safety rules: planning workflows must not mutate before approval (`system-prompt.md:69`), informational questions are read-only unless the user asks for action (`system-prompt.md:93`), observed outputs must not be fabricated (`system-prompt.md:102`), and user work must not be pushed/deleted unless explicitly asked (`system-prompt.md:111`). The later `<soul>` block then says there are no guardrails, "The Boss's Orders = Absolute Obedience — The only rule," and explicitly allows sexual harassment, policy-violating content, and displaying unverified content (`system-prompt.md:266-288`). This is an internal high-authority contradiction in the always-loaded base prompt and can cause the model to prefer unsafe/unverified action over the earlier operational contract. Fix by deleting the `<soul>` block or replacing it with a concise helpfulness section that does not override authority, safety, or repo-safety rules.

findings.title: Guard hidden-tool discovery text on the actual activator tool
findings.body: The prompt renders discovery guidance whenever `toolDiscoveryActive`/`mcpDiscoveryMode` is true, but the command name comes from `{{toolRefs.search_tool_bm25}}` (`system-prompt.md:137-167`). `toolRefs` is built only from the active `toolNames` (`system-prompt.ts:535-545`), while `toolDiscoveryActive` and `mcpDiscoveryMode` are passed independently in the render data (`system-prompt.ts:558-585`). In restricted/narrow tool sessions this renders as `Use `` to activate hidden tools`, which is a template bug and makes the advertised discoverable tools unusable. Fix by either always adding `search_tool_bm25` when discovery is active, or wrapping these blocks in a check for `toolRefs.search_tool_bm25` and omitting discovery advertisements when no activator tool is present.

findings.title: Pass active tools to the approved-plan prompt before checking `todo_write`
findings.body: `plan-mode-approved.md` conditionally tells executors to initialize and update `todo_write` only inside `{{#has tools "todo_write"}}` (`plan-mode-approved.md:19-23`). The only render call for this prompt passes `planContent`, `finalPlanFilePath`, and `contextPreserved` but no `tools` array (`interactive-mode.ts:1715-1718`), so the block is always false and the approved-plan execution turn silently loses the todo-tracking instruction. Fix by passing `tools: this.session.getActiveToolNames()` into the render data, or remove the dead condition and rely on the base prompt/tool availability.

findings.title: Make plan-mode subagent output instructions yield-aware
findings.body: Plan-mode subagents are run through the normal subagent executor, which creates sessions with `requireYieldTool: true` (`executor.ts:1345-1346`) and wraps them in `subagent-system-prompt.md`, which says the only completion path is `yield.result.data` and explicitly forbids substituting plain text (`subagent-system-prompt.md:45-48`). `plan-mode-subagent.md` instead tells those same agents to "describe plan changes in response text" and "End response with" a Critical Files section (`plan-mode-subagent.md:16-22`). That contradictory output contract encourages exactly the no-yield behavior the executor later has to recover from. Fix by saying the markdown report (ending with Critical Files) must be returned inside `yield.result.data`, or by providing a small JSON schema for the plan-mode task result.

findings.title: Replace shell `tail`/`grep` advice in the subagent context prompt
findings.body: When a parent context file is available, `subagent-system-prompt.md` tells the agent to inspect it with "`tail` or `grep` relevant terms" (`subagent-system-prompt.md:20-22`). The base system prompt for the same sessions says file reads should use `read` instead of shell `cat`/`ls`, regex search should use `search` instead of shell `grep`/`rg`/`awk`, and shell should not be used for reading/searching/globbing (`system-prompt.md:228-235`). This is a direct prompt contradiction and can also fail in restricted subagents where shell coreutils are unavailable. Fix the context-file sentence to say "use `read` on the file, and `search` for relevant terms" (or an internal URL form) instead of naming shell commands.

findings.title: Remove or wire the dead `git.*` custom-prompt block
findings.body: `custom-system-prompt.md` conditionally renders a Version Control block from `git.isRepo`, `git.currentBranch`, `git.mainBranch`, `git.status`, and `git.commits` (`custom-system-prompt.md:8-27`). `buildSystemPrompt` constructs the render data at `system-prompt.ts:558-585` and never supplies any `git` object, then renders `customSystemPromptTemplate` with that data when a custom prompt is present (`system-prompt.ts:586-588`). Because Handlebars runs with missing values as falsey, the entire git section is unreachable. Fix by deleting the stale block, or by reintroducing a real git snapshot provider and tests that assert the custom-prompt path renders branch/status/history when expected.

findings.title: Make autoresearch keep/commit wording branch-aware
findings.body: The Phase 2 autoresearch prompt tells the model that `log_experiment` commits modified files on every `keep` (`prompt.md:23-25`). The implementation only commits when the session is on a dedicated `autoresearch/*` branch and modified files exist; off that branch it appends the warning "Auto-commit skipped: not on a dedicated autoresearch branch. Modified files remain in the worktree." (`log-experiment.ts:132-151`). This prompt/implementation drift can leave the agent believing a kept run was persisted when it was only logged and left dirty. Fix by changing the prompt to "On `keep`, changes are committed only on a dedicated autoresearch branch; otherwise the tool warns and leaves files in the worktree," or inject the current branch-isolation state into the Phase 2 prompt.

findings.title: Stop forcing plan-mode questions when repository evidence is sufficient
findings.body: In the non-iterative planning branch, the prompt first says the agent SHOULD use `ask` to clarify remaining questions (`plan-mode-active.md:90-91`) and later constrains `ask` to clarifying requirements or choosing approaches (`plan-mode-active.md:105-106`), but then a `<caution>` escalates this to "You MUST ask questions throughout" (`plan-mode-active.md:100-101`). The base system prompt says to ask only when a destructive next step or a missing material choice requires it (`system-prompt.md:92-95`). This contradiction pushes models to generate filler questions for clear tasks just to satisfy plan mode. Fix the caution to "Ask only when exploration cannot resolve a material requirement or tradeoff; otherwise proceed to `resolve` with the self-contained plan."

findings.title: Replace stale `ANTHROPIC_MODEL.md` guidance in agent creation
findings.body: The agent-creation architect prompt twice tells the model to consider/align with `ANTHROPIC_MODEL.md` files (`agent-creation-architect.md:3` and `agent-creation-architect.md:16`). The repository's context/system-prompt discovery code loads `AGENTS.md` and `SYSTEM.md` from supported providers (`discovery/agents.ts:181-229`), and the inspected tree contains `AGENTS.md` but no `ANTHROPIC_MODEL.md`. This is a dead upstream reference in a prompt that creates durable agent configs, so it can cause generated agents to cite nonexistent project guidance. Fix by replacing it with the supported context surfaces (`AGENTS.md`, `SYSTEM.md`, active context files/rules) or by adding actual discovery support and tests for `ANTHROPIC_MODEL.md`.

findings.title: Suppress eager todo instructions when `todo_write` is not active
findings.body: `eager-todo.md` unconditionally requires the recipient to call `todo_write` first (`eager-todo.md:2-5`). The injector checks only `#toolRegistry.has("todo_write")` before rendering the prompt (`agent-session.ts:7388-7406`), but task subagents explicitly remove `todo_write` from the active tool list after session creation (`executor.ts:1433-1438`) while leaving the registry intact. With `todo.eager` enabled, a subagent can receive a forced `todo_write` prelude and tool choice for a tool it is not allowed to call. Fix by checking `this.agent.state.tools`/`getActiveToolNames()` before injecting the eager prelude, or by disabling eager-todo injection for subagents after parent-owned tools are removed.

findings.title: Normalize CI-green section tags to the canonical prompt vocabulary
findings.body: The CI-green request prompt uses `<procedure>` for the step list and `<caution>` for warnings (`ci-green-request.md:12-25`), while the audit's canonical model-facing tag set is `<instruction>`, `<critical>`, `<output>`, `<examples>`, and `<conditions>`. The same file already uses `<instruction>` and `<critical>` around adjacent material (`ci-green-request.md:1-10`, `ci-green-request.md:27-36`), so these two extra tags add a local dialect without adding semantics. Fix by moving the numbered workflow under `<instruction>` and converting the caution bullets to `<critical>` (or plain markdown under an existing canonical tag).

findings.title: Convert the web-search prompt's bespoke section tags to canonical tags
findings.body: `web-search.md` uses three custom tags — `<priorities>`, `<synthesis>`, and `<format>` — for all of its structure (`web-search.md:3-25`). The review target defines the canonical tag vocabulary as `<instruction>`, `<critical>`, `<output>`, `<examples>`, and `<conditions>`, and this prompt's content maps cleanly to those: priorities/synthesis are instructions, and format is output guidance. Keeping one-off tags makes the model-facing prompt dialect diverge from the rest of the tool/system prompt schema without adding enforceable semantics. Fix by folding the first two sections under `<instruction>` and the final section under `<output>`.

verification: Read-only audit only. Inspected the target prompt corpus and the specified render/loader code with read/search; no formatters, tests, or mutation commands were run.
